title: Lumbini Province - The Kathmandu Post
authors: By Durgalal KC & Jaya Singh Mahara
summary: Butwal launches drive to clear encroached footpaths and roadside structures By Ghana Shyam Gautam Municipality begins removing shops, parked vehicles and illegal structures from major commercial areas. No action on probe report months after Bardiya father-daughter deaths By Ram Prasad Chauhan Investigation had flagged lapses in a citizenship denial case, but disciplinary and legal steps remain pen
content:
Butwal launches drive to clear encroached footpaths and roadside structures By Ghana Shyam Gautam Municipality begins removing shops, parked vehicles and illegal structures from major commercial areas. No action on probe report months after Bardiya father-daughter deaths By Ram Prasad Chauhan Investigation had flagged lapses in a citizenship denial case, but disciplinary and legal steps remain pending despite the findings having been submitted to the government. Fire destroys 22 houses and sheds in western Nepal, causes Rs9.35 million loss By Shamsher Bikram GC Blaze in Pyuthan village kills livestock and destroys property before being contained by security forces and locals. Tiger conservation leaves families on forest fringes in peril By Rupa Gahatraj Between physical disability and the crushing weight of debt, survivors of tiger attacks in Banke and Bardiya are left to fend for themselves. After six-year delay, Vishwa Shanti Dham construction resumes in Lumbini By Ghanashyam Gautam A Rs5-billion meditation facility shaped like a blooming lotus will house a museum, library and retreat centres. Locals demand entry ban at Tinau dam after repeated drownings By Post Report Calls grow for stricter restrictions as two Indian teenagers go missing, with fatalities continuing despite repeated warnings. Kapilvastu remains under prohibitory order as calm returns slowly By Manoj Paudel Authorities say normalcy is gradually returning in Maharajganj after a community dispute triggered violence and left 23 injured, but restrictions remain in force as talks continue. RSP’s Bikram Khanal stuns Binod Chaudhary in Nawalparasi West-1 By Post Report Khanal wins with 45,241 votes, defeating former lawmaker and billionaire Chaudhary by 35,739 votes. RSP candidate Panthi takes early lead in Gulmi-2 By Post Report Congress and UML candidates close behind as early vote count shows tight race. RSP candidate Kharel widens lead in Rupandehi-2 By Post Report UML Vice-chair Bishnu Paudel trails by over 13,000 votes in latest count. NCP’s Barshaman Pun takes early lead in Rolpa By Post Report Initial counting shows a close contest with Maoist candidate Chudamani Oli trailing narrowly. RSP’s Kamal Subedi takes early lead in Dang-3 By Post Report Subedi ahead of rivals as initial votes are counted. Vote counting to begin in Gulmi from 10am By Santosh Mahatara Over half of registered voters cast ballots in both constituencies, with women leading participation. Pushpa Kamal Dahal takes early lead in Rukum East By Post Report Nepali Communist Party candidate ahead in preliminary count, followed by CPN-UML and Congress. Counting begins in Rukum East By Post Report Pushpa Kamal Dahal, Lilamani Gautam and Kusum Devi Thapa in the fray. Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Khagendra Sunar leads in Banke-3 By Rupa Gahatraj Sunar ahead of UML and Congress rivals in early vote count. Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Devraj Pathak leads in Dang-1 By Durga Lal KC Pathak ahead of Congress and UML candidates as early vote count begins. Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Sulabh Kharel leads in Rupandehi-2 By Post Report Kharel ahead of UML’s Bishnu Paudel in early vote count. No votes cast at two polling centres in Dang-3 By Durgalal KC So far, no ballots have been cast at the Deuti Secondary School Tallo Malaikhola polling centre in Babai Rural Municipality-7 and the Janata Basic School Jila polling centre in Babai-1. Politicians return every election, but promises of land titles remain unfulfilled By Durgalal KC & Jaya Singh Mahara Freed Kamaiyas in Sani Ambapur, Dang, say for years they have been paying taxes on land they do not legally own.

By Ghana Shyam Gautam
